title: RS-MCA Cycle 87 Proof

assistants.text: I’ll reconstruct the Cycle 84–87 reduction chain from the supplied context, then stress-test the 464 fusion, affine normalization, multiplicity certificate, and field-size target at their first fragile implications.

The leading concern is whether the two witnesses truly share one global six-jet syndrome, rather than consuming separate reserves; the 560 fallback looks algebraically coherent but still needs quotient/color-count auditing.

The 464 fusion now survives direct one-code/one-line reconstruction via a 232-row GRS parity check, so the remaining attack is hidden projective multiplicity: quotient symmetries or two-slot relations causing a ninefold collision.

The shortening issue has narrowed to terminology: the repaired parity-check restriction preserves dimension and transversality. I’m now seeking a smaller projected census that would imply the full multiplicity bound.

# COMMON PROMPT FOR CYCLE 87

Try to fully solve the problem. If you cannot fully solve it, progress it as
much as possible. No Internet. Take all the time to reason you need. Use MAX
reasoning.

You are working on the RS-MCA / proximity-prize finite obstruction route. Do
not brainstorm from scratch. Read the attached context first, especially:

- CYCLE87_CURRENT_STATE.md
- m1_cycle86_two_copy_affine_color_returns_audit.md
- m1_cycle85_role05_transfer_returns_audit.md
- m1_cycle84_wallbreaker_returns_audit.md
- the Cycle86 raw returns if you need to inspect a worker's exact claim

The active target is:


text
L-CYCLE86-EXPLICIT-TWO-COPY-SEPARATOR-CERTIFICATE


Prove, with a replayable certificate, that a concrete separator y for the
two-copy construction has projective multiplicity at most 8 and that the
resulting supports form one transverse affine syndrome line of a single
official-rate GRS code.

Equivalent checker wall:
